subject: [CONTRIB] Introduce certificate update use case in CertService
commit_message:
[CONTRIB] Introduce certificate update use case in CertService

1. Make changes in order to allow performing KUR/CR in EJBCA:
- Add Certificate Update Admin role
- Enable EndEntityAuthentication module
- Create and set CA with constant UID
- Add configuration for provider.
2. Update CertService, which provides with new certificate update
endpoint.
3. Update release-notes.

Issue-ID: OOM-2753
Issue-ID: OOM-2754
Signed-off-by: Piotr Marcinkiewicz <piotr.marcinkiewicz@nokia.com>
Change-Id: I9cb0cb4d6d6939ad229a4ea254f2bc35d45a3d52
Signed-off-by: Joanna Jeremicz <joanna.jeremicz@nokia.com>

Patch Set 13:

@Sylvain

I have heard that you have some doubts about manual upgrading CRD.
Unfortunately it looks that helm 3 doesn't support upgrade CRD in any way.

In the official documentation we can find that: "These CRDs are not templated, but will be installed by default when running a helm install for the chart. If the CRD already exists, it will be skipped with a warning." (https://helm.sh/docs/chart_best_practices/custom_resource_definitions/)

So, this is independent of the version we specified in CRD (v1 or v2) - "helm install" detects that CRD exists and skips installing the new one. Helm 3 also doesn't manage CRD's lifecycle, so it doesn't delete it when uninstalling the chart.

It is worth mentioning that Kubernetes correctly handles the new CRD after deploying the old one. It is sufficient to execute kubectl apply, without removing the old CRD first. It confirms that adding a new optional field (updateEndpoint) doesn't break CMPv2Issuer CRD API and the change is backwards compatible, so it isn't necessary to change CRD version from v1 to v2.
